entry.process: Fly the Q flag on arrival; captain clears in at the nearest port of entry with boat papers + passports (crew stays aboard until cleared). Apply via the Click2Clear portal to speed it up.
entry.permit: Cruising permit (fees effective Apr 2026): 30 days $150–$600 by length (≤50 ft = $250); 6 months ≤50 ft $300 (one free reentry); 12 months ≤50 ft $500 (two free reentries).
entry.max_stay: Visitors typically granted up to 8 months; cruising permits available to 12 months.

money: Bahamian dollar (BSD), pegged 1:1 to USD — US dollars accepted everywhere.
fishing: Fishing permit is separate from the cruising permit: $100 (<50 ft) / $300 (≥50 ft), valid 30 days, renewable. Spearfishing: free-diving only with Hawaiian sling or pole spear — no spearguns (illegal to possess), no scuba take, nothing within 200 yd of settled islands. Lobster season Aug 1 – Mar 31. Anchorage fees for foreign vessels are also separate ($50+ by length/duration).
notes: VHF 16 monitored; BASRA (volunteer) handles sea rescue out of Nassau.
Hurricane season Jun–Nov — many insurers require a named-storm plan or being out of the box.
Provision hard in Nassau or George Town; out-island fuel, water and groceries are scarce and pricey.
Banks/ATMs only on bigger islands — carry cash for the out islands.
sources: Bahamas Customs / Southern Boating fee schedule (Apr 2026)
